purpose.summary: This BMS map defines the screen layout for the 'CardDemo - Pending Authorization Screen'. It is used to display pending credit card authorizations and related customer information, allowing users to search by account ID and select transactions for further action.
purpose.business_context: This screen is part of a credit card authorization workflow, likely used by customer service representatives or fraud analysts to review and manage pending transactions.

inputs.name: ACCTID
inputs.description: Account ID entered by the user to search for pending authorizations.

outputs.name: COPAU0A
outputs.description: The entire screen layout, including static text, customer information, and a list of pending transactions.

paragraphs.purpose: This DFHMSD macro defines the BMS map set 'COPAU00'. It specifies overall characteristics of the map set, including control options (ALARM, FREEKB), extended attributes (EXTATT=YES), the programming language (LANG=COBOL), input/output mode (MODE=INOUT), storage allocation (STORAGE=AUTO), terminal I/O area prefix (TIOAPFX=YES), and the map type based on the system parameter (TYPE=&&SYSPARM). This macro acts as the container for all the individual map definitions within this map set. It essentially initializes the screen definition process by setting the stage for defining the individual fields and their attributes that will be displayed on the terminal. The specified attributes influence how the screen interacts with the user and the CICS environment. It doesn't directly consume or produce data but sets the framework for subsequent map definitions to handle data input and output.
paragraphs.dead_code_reason: Artifact 'COPAU00' (map) is never referenced by any other artifact in the dependency graph

paragraphs.purpose: This DFHMDI macro defines an individual map named 'COPAU0A' within the 'COPAU00' map set. It specifies the map's position on the screen (COLUMN=1, LINE=1) and its size (SIZE=(24,80)), effectively defining a full-screen map. This macro acts as a container for the individual field definitions (DFHMDF macros) that make up the screen layout. It establishes the boundaries within which the screen elements will be arranged. The map definition itself doesn't directly interact with data; instead, it provides the structure and dimensions for the screen that will be used to display information and accept user input. It sets the stage for the subsequent DFHMDF macros to define the specific fields and their attributes within this screen area. The map is designed to occupy the entire screen, providing ample space for displaying customer information and pending transaction details.
paragraphs.dead_code_reason: Screen/Map 'COPAU0A' is never sent to or received from by any program

open_questions.question: What is the purpose of the '&&SYSPARM' variable used in the TYPE parameter of the DFHMSD macro?
open_questions.context: The code uses 'TYPE=&&SYSPARM' without explaining where this variable is defined or what values it can take.
open_questions.suggestion: Check the JCL or CICS definitions associated with this BMS map to determine the possible values and their impact on the map type (e.g., DSECT, MAP, etc.).
